Lil Tay finally dropped the link everyone’s been waiting for, sort of.
On the heels of supposedly launching her OnlyFans account, the 18-year-old online personality has released a new single, “Stuck in July,” on Tuesday (August 5).
The summery soft-pop single and its accompanying music video are completely safe for work, unlike the adult content she teased earlier this week.
“Since I’m the generous princess and no one else dropped song of the summer,” Tay wrote on her X account.
“I saw y’all praying the link was new music…
STUCK IN JULY is out now on youtube.
After Tay became a victim to a death hoax in 2023, she launched a music career with the pop banger “Sucker 4 Green” and followed up with a second single, “Growing Up,” the following year.
“Just learned through instagram that Lil Tay lied about actually becoming an OF creator and just used the account to bait people into watching/hearing new music she released,” wrote one user.
